channel.title: Off-Mic with Asha Gallegos
channel.description: Long-form conversations with the people who make South African media. One guest, roughly forty-five minutes, the story behind the story. Hosted by Asha Gallegos in Melville, Johannesburg.
channel.show:author: Asha Gallegos

item.title: Pilot: Why this show exists
item.description: A short opening note on what Off-Mic is, why it is built independently, and who it is for.

item.title: A voice that belongs to no platform
item.description: On building a public voice that outlives any single newsroom, and what independence really asks of a broadcaster.

item.title: What a long investigation costs
item.description: The years, the risk, and the quiet toll of the reporting that nobody sees while it is being done.

item.title: Writing identity out loud
item.description: Memoir, public writing, and the weight of being asked to represent more than yourself.

item.title: The archive of a nation's taste
item.description: Music journalism and how a country remembers its own sound across generations.
